hypothesis: If metal–hydrogen candidates are generated from metal lattice templates with strong interstitial (quasi-atom) electron localization and screened by a stack-ensemble ML model trained on lattice/electronic features, then the prioritized compounds will be thermodynamically stable (low Ehull) and include high-Tc superconductors under pressure because these features capture the 3D electronic connectivity and hydrogen-derived density of states that control stability and electron–phonon coupling.
